title: acaena torilicarpa
intro: Acaena torilicarpa, also known as the hairy cat's ear, is a perennial herb in the rose family that is native to New Zealand and Australia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Acaena torilicarpa include the potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and eczema. Acaena torilicarpa can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
